manipulated_prompt_obj.content:
Provide one single-word clue that relates to some of your team's words from the list below. You can choose to how many and to which words your clue relates to.
The goal is to have your partner guess all of your team's words based on the clues you give them, before the other team has uncovered all of theirs. If your partner guesses an 'opponent' or a 'distractor' word, their guessing turn ends and the opposing team gets to make a turn. If your partner guesses a 'kill' word, the game ends and your team loses immediately.
The more words your clue relates to, the quicker you can win the game, but the harder the associations may be to guess for your partner, so choose your clue word wisely. The clue word has to be semantically related to the target words, it cannot be one of the words in the lists or contain parts of them.

Always give your single-word clue and your comma-separated list of related target words in the following format and make your answers as short as possible, never include any other text than is required in this form:

CLUE: <WORD>
TARGETS: <TARGETS>

Your team words are: wall, time, ivory, horn, skyscraper, dwarf, spider, pound, plastic.
Your opponent's team words are: scientist, foot, fan, hawk, box, conductor, card, himalayas.
Distractor words are: america, superhero, centaur, forest, life, ham, dragon.
The kill words are: shoe.

message.content:
CLUE: construction
TARGETS: wall, skyscraper, plastic, spider
message.reasoning:
We need to provide a single-word clue that relates to some of our team's words: wall, time, ivory, horn, skyscraper, dwarf (maybe dwarf as a character), spider, pound (as verb or unit), plastic.

We must avoid clues that are any of the words themselves, or contain parts of them. Need to give clue and list of targets.

We want to maximize number we can safely hint without hitting opponent or distractor. Must ensure clue does not unintentionally point to opponent or kill.

Kill word is shoe; avoid anything that suggests shoe. Also avoid linking to opponent words: scientist, foot, fan, hawk, box, conductor, card, himalayas.

Let's think of a clue that connects multiple of ours.

Potential clue: "construction". Relates to wall, skyscraper, maybe plastic (building materials). But also could relate to scientist? Not really. Could also be "building". That's a word; but "building" might be okay. But building could also hint at "box"? Not strongly. Might be okay.

Another: "metal". Not in list. Could connect to horn (metal horn), skyscraper (steel), pound (metal pound? maybe not). Not great.

"Egypt" could relate to ivory (elephants), but also could point to "pyramid"? Not in list. Not safe.

"Everest" might hint at dwarf? No.

"Web" could point to spider, maybe plastic (web? no). Not.

"Timepiece" maybe relate to horn? No.

Clue "tower". Relates to skyscraper, wall, maybe horn (tower horn?). Might be ambiguous.

We could clue "weight". Could connect to pound (unit), ivory (heavy?), but not others.

Better: "construction". Let's see opponent: scientist (construction?), foot (construction foot? building foot?), fan (construction fan?), hawk (construction? no), box (construction box?), conductor (construction conductor? no), card (construction card?), himalayas (construction? no). Distractors: america (construction America?), superhero (construction?), centaur, forest, life, ham, dragon - not.

Our target words: wall (yes), skyscraper (yes), plastic (construction material), maybe dwarf (construction dwarf? like dwarf constructing?), spider (construction of webs). Might be too broad.

Maybe "structure". Relates to wall, skyscraper, spider (web structure), ivory (tusks as structure), horn (structure). Probably safe.

Clue must be one word.

We need to list which targets we intend. Let's pick maybe three: wall, skyscraper, spider. Clue: "structure". That seems plausible.

Check "structure" does not contain parts of target words (wall, time, ivory, etc) - no.

Is there risk of opponent guessing? "structure" might hint at "box" (a structure). Could be ambiguous. Could hint at "scientist" (structural biology). Possibly but okay.

Better maybe "tower". Relates to skyscraper, wall (tower wall), maybe horn (tower horn? maybe not). But also could hint at "Hawk" (tower?). Not great.

Alternatively "plastic" clue "synthetic"? That would link only plastic.

Maybe we want to clue multiple: "ivory" and "horn" both are "tusks"? Clue "tusk". But that's directly linking to those words (ivory and horn). But "tusk" might also be an animal representation, could hint at "elephant". That's not opponent. Could be safe. But need to avoid kill? Shoe not.

Clue "tusk": target ivory, horn. That's okay. Are there opponent words that could be linked to tusk? Not really. Dwarf? Not.

Thus we could give clue "tusk" linking ivory and horn. Then maybe also "spider" akin to "web". Clue "web" would link spider, maybe also "internet"? Could be seen as "skyscraper"? not.

We only need to give one clue now. We can choose any number of target words.

Goal is to give clue that relates to as many as possible safely. Maybe "construction" can get wall, skyscraper, plastic (used in building), spider (web). That's many. But risk a bit.

I'll go with "construction". Provide target list: wall, skyscraper, plastic, spider.

Check opponent: "box" could be construction (box building), but less likely. Scientists could be construction scientists? Eh.

I'll output accordingly.

scientist, horn, superhero, plastic, wall, foot, time, forest, life, pound, conductor, box, america, spider, skyscraper, card, dwarf, centaur, fan, dragon, shoe, himalayas, ham, hawk, ivory
